note: Two compose calls. Call 1 is the plan-mandated pin (deck-product-vision) which failed UNKNOWN_TEMPLATE — that world was never templatized. Call 2 re-pins the SAME grammar's live template (tminus / deck-product-launch, descriptor grammarId 'monumental-type') so the specimen still renders genuine monumental-type pixels. Divergence documented in notes.md.

request.contentBrief: Introduce OpenDesign, an internal design-system platform: one design system compiled into five composable surfaces (slide decks, dashboards, project pages, personal pages, technical explainers) rendered as 65 live gallery worlds. The deck explains the division-of-labor doctrine — world-templates carry all the craft (layout, color, motion) while typed fills carry only content — the COMPOSE flow from source docs to a validated fill via MCP tools (deterministic template selection, fill skeleton, validate_fill loop, max 3 rounds), the skill/MCP handshake, the three routes (COMPOSE a whole experience, BORROW one part via public data-part-id contracts, AUDIT design quality against gate files), and the quality loop (certifier to 0 findings, part-id tests, verify rig, screenshot judge) proven in the July 2026 pilot runs.

error.message: pinTemplateId "deck-product-vision" is not a live slide-deck template.
details: Live slide-deck templates: cutover (deck-cloud-migration), dgm-blueprint (deck-dgm-blueprint), dgm-circuit (deck-dgm-circuit), dgm-gazette (deck-dgm-gazette), dgm-isometric (deck-dgm-isometric), dgm-sketchnote (deck-dgm-sketchnote), quarter (deck-quarterly-business-review), tminus (deck-product-launch).
remediation: Pin one of this surface's template ids/experienceIds (typically from a previous call's alternatives), or drop pinTemplateId to let scoring decide.

result.rationale: Pinned 'tminus' (deck-product-launch, art-directed style) by explicit pinTemplateId — scoring bypassed; score shown for transparency.

guidance: A launch plan staged as a COUNTDOWN SEQUENCE: every slide carries a monumental T-minus stamp (T-30 → T-0) over a midnight field, with a single thin amber horizon line that RISES slide by slide and, at T-0, reaches the top as the field turns GO-green.

Exactly one readiness gate carries the "warning" flagged-blocker status: the single amber square in an otherwise green board, its verbatim text echoed in anomalyLabel under the board and in the accessible summary.
The synthetic-launch notice is required and prints on every slide’s print foot and the footer.
The day-0 runbook is the commanding bespoke visual — a local SVG rail with a go/no-go diamond gate — and exposes a hidden ordered-list mirror derived from the same runbook data.
Slot char caps and item counts are sized so any schema-valid fill stays composed — the runbook rail, the price grid, and the readiness board stay balanced and headlines never overflow.
Motion level 2 (staggered build + horizon-sweep); the mood is locked dark.

fillSkeleton.note: Verbatim per-slot skeleton omitted here for size; it is byte-identical to the descriptor view compiled from TMINUS_SECTIONS in experiences/slide-decks/deck-product-launch/tminus-fill.ts (10 sections: title, one-sentence, thesis, readiness, comms, pricing, runbook, risk, metrics, closing — each repeats exactly 1; slot names, types, caps and guidance as in that file), plus a descriptor-drawn example per slot from the shipped MERIDIAN INSTANT fill.
